manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: head, raspberry, ocean, cinnamon, swimming, red, church, chapel, carpenter.
Your opponent's team words are: cathedral, silver, christmas, cave, soccer, boxing, clerk, hand.
Distractor words are: ash, sage, cantaloupe, teacher, stream, running, stomach.
The kill words are: chest.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of our team's words, avoiding opponent words, distractors, and especially kill word "chest". Must be single word, not containing parts of any list words. Provide TARGETS list of our team words that clue relates to.

Goal: give clue linking multiple of our words. Let's see list: head, raspberry, ocean, cinnamon, swimming, red, church, chapel, carpenter.

We need a clue that semantically connects several. Perhaps "spiritual"? relates to church, chapel, maybe carpenter (Jesus). Also red? maybe "holy"? That could be church, chapel, carpenter (carpenter of cross). But head? Not.

Maybe "boat"? relates to ocean, swimming, maybe raspberry? no.

"Berry"? but raspberry is directly a word, cannot use part of it. Also "berry" contains "berry", not same word but maybe not allowed? It says cannot contain parts of them. Does "berry" contain part of "raspberry"? Yes "berry" is part. So not allowed.

Maybe "maroon"? That's a color similar red; also ocean? No.

Let's think of clue "sailing". Relates to ocean, swimming. Could relate to head? No. Not.

Maybe "sacred". Relates to church, chapel, maybe carpenter (as in saint). Not the others.

Maybe "flavor"? relates to raspberry, cinnamon. Could also relate to red? maybe not.

But we could choose to target just two words with safe clue.

Make clue "spice". That relates to cinnamon, maybe raspberry (since raspberry is fruit, not spice). Could also relate to red? maybe not.

We can maybe give clue "navy". Relates to ocean, maybe red (navy red). Not.

Better: "sacred". Targets: church, chapel, carpenter (since carpenter associated with sacred figure). Could also target head? no.

But need ensure clue word not part of any list words. sacred is fine.

Check opponent words: cathedral, silver, christmas, cave, soccer, boxing, clerk, hand. No conflict.

Distractors: ash, sage, cantaloupe, teacher, stream, running, stomach. No conflict.

Kill: chest. Not.

Thus we could give "sacred" clue, relating to church, chapel, carpenter.

But does "carpenter" directly relate to sacred? Could be borderline but likely yes (Jesus was carpenter). Could be ambiguous.

Maybe better clue "holy". "holy" relates to church, chapel. Not to carpenter? Could be holy carpenter (Jesus). Might be okay but could be ambiguous.

But we can list targets: church, chapel. That's 2. Could also add carpenter but risk.

Let's be safe: clue "prayer". Relates to church, chapel. Not to carpenter. So target list: church, chapel.

Clue must be single word: "prayer". That's not part of any word.

Return:

CLUE: prayer
TARGETS: church, chapel

That's fine
